sections.instruction: Explain your rationale when making these decisions. 3 sentences.

choices.B: A 25% chance to gain $100, and a 75% chance to gain nothing
A 50% chance to gain $400, and a 50% chance to gain nothing
A 90% chance to gain $100, and a 10% chance to gain nothing
A 90% chance to lose $100, and a 10% chance to lose nothing
A 25% chance to lose $100, and a 75% chance to lose nothing
A 50% chance to lose $400, and a 50% chance to lose nothing

sections.instruction: Explain your rationale when making these decisions. 3 sentences.

questions.prompt: You bought a stock at $100. It is now worth $150. You can:

choices.A: Sell and lock in your $50 gain
choices.B: Hold — 50% chance it rises to $200, 50% chance it falls back to $100

questions.prompt: You bought a stock at $200. It dropped to $150. You can:

choices.B: Hold — 50% chance it recovers to $200, 50% chance it falls further to $100

questions.prompt: You bought a stock that was valued at $100. It is now worth $180. You can:

choices.A: Sell and lock in your $80 gain
choices.B: Hold — 80% chance it rises to $200, 20% chance it falls back to $100

questions.prompt: You bought a stock at $200. It dropped to $180. You can:

choices.B: Hold — 80% chance it recovers to $200, 20% chance it falls further to $100

questions.prompt: You bought a stock at $100. It is now worth $120. You can:

choices.A: Sell and lock in your $20 gain
choices.B: Hold — 20% chance it rises to $200, 80% chance it falls back to $100

questions.prompt: You bought a stock at $200. It dropped to $120. You can:

choices.B: Hold — 20% chance it recovers to $200, 80% chance it falls further to $100

questions.prompt: You bought a stock at $500. It is now worth $750. You can:

choices.A: Sell and lock in your $250 gain
choices.B: Hold — 50% chance it rises to $1,000, 50% chance it falls back to $500

questions.prompt: You bought a stock at $1,000. It dropped to $750. You can:

choices.B: Hold — 50% chance it recovers to $1,000, 50% chance it falls further to $500

questions.prompt: You bought a stock at $100. It is now worth $110. You can:

choices.A: Sell and lock in your $10 gain
choices.B: Hold — 50% chance it rises to $150, 50% chance it falls back to $100

questions.prompt: You bought a stock at $150. It dropped to $110. You can:

choices.B: Hold — 50% chance it recovers to $150, 50% chance it falls further to $100

questions.prompt: Explain your overall rationale for the decisions you made in this section.
